title: taraxacum mucronulatum
intro: Taraxacum mucronulatum, also known as the Korean dandelion or Japanese dandelion, is a plant species within the dandelion family, native to East Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve liver and gallbladder issues. The health benefits associated with Taraxacum mucronulatum include improved liver function, reduced cholesterol levels, and enhanced immune system function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in tea, capsule, or tincture form, and are often used to support liver health and overall well-being.
